input: The customer should really understand. Exactly what they require without the need to have to talk to a contact centre. By the time they get to a contact centre, often it's too late for them to be able to. Get what the answer that they want. A way that is decent and good for the person.
expectedUnits: Customers should understand what they need without having to contact a contact centre.
By the time a customer reaches a contact centre, it is often already too late to get the right answer.

expectedDiscarded.text: A way that is decent and good for the person.

note: Adjacent clauses 1+2 merge into one business meaning. Clauses 3+4 merge into a second. Clause 5 is discarded.

input: I think that things can be resolved without even a conversation with customers and actually be the direction we go. It's really fixing issues upstream rather than downstream.
expectedUnits: Issues should be resolved upstream without requiring a customer conversation.
note: The second sentence reinforces and completes the first; they should form one unit.

input: Agents need help spotting vulnerability in a customer. AI could identify that vulnerability and guide the agent on how to handle it, including escalating the call when needed.
expectedUnits: AI could help agents identify customer vulnerability and guide the right response, including escalation when needed.
note: One coherent claim; should not be split into detection and escalation separately.

input: Technology tends to fail a lot. And if the technology keeps failing in a contact centre, then operations are gonna fail.
expectedUnits: When technology repeatedly fails in a contact centre, operations fail as a result.
note: Two clauses form one causal unit.

input: Um, yeah, I mean, you know, kind of.

expectedDiscarded.text: Um, yeah, I mean, you know, kind of.

input: I can see it becoming the intelligence hub of the organisation.

expectedDiscarded.text: I can see it becoming the intelligence hub of the organisation.

note: The subject of 'it' is unresolved, so no unit may be emitted.

input: By the time they reach an agent, the problem has got sufficiently big that they can't fix it without the help of a person.
expectedUnits: By the time a customer reaches an agent, the problem is often too significant to be resolved without human help.

input: It was like a mini massage.
